turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Mom missed her cardiology appointment yesterday because no one picked her up.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I thought you were taking her. You put it on the group calendar.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I put it there so everyone could see it. I did not say I was taking her.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: The last three appointments, you took her. So I assumed.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: That's exactly the problem. Everyone assumes I will handle it.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I am not "everyone." I have two kids and a job that doesn't let me leave in the middle of the day.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I also have a job. I just rearrange mine because if I don't, things fall apart.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: That sounds like you're saying I don't care about Mom.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I'm saying I need you to stop acting like your schedule is real and mine is flexible.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I never said your schedule isn't real.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: You don't have to say it. That's how the whole family operates.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Here we go, now it's the whole family.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Because it is. Aunt Carla called me this morning asking why I "let" Mom miss the appointment. Nobody called you.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I didn't ask Aunt Carla to call you.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: But you benefit from it. I'm the default daughter.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: That is not fair. I paid the pharmacy bill last month.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: And then you mentioned it three times like it proved you were doing your part.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Because it was four hundred dollars, Nina.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I know. I said thank you.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: You said thank you and then immediately said money is the easy part.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Because for me, time is the expensive part.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Okay. I can see that.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Can you? Because when I ask for help, you say you're busy, and then I feel guilty for asking.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I don't want you to feel guilty. I just need more notice.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: The appointment was on the calendar for two weeks.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: But nobody assigned it to me.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Why do I have to assign you tasks like you're a teenager?

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Because if you need me to do something, tell me directly.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I need you to look at the calendar and volunteer before I am drowning.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I can try, but I don't think I'll notice everything the way you do.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: That sounds like another way to make it my job.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: No, it is me being honest about what I can realistically do.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Realistically, I am burning out.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I hear that.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Do you? Or are you just saying that so I calm down?

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I am saying it because I hear you. You are carrying too much.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Thank you. I needed you to say that without arguing.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I should have said it sooner.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I also need to be honest. When you pay for things, I sometimes dismiss it because I am angry about the time. That's not fair to you.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I appreciate that. I do feel like the money part disappears unless I bring it up.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: So both things are true. You contribute financially, and I am doing too much coordination.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Yes. I can agree with that.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Then we need a system, not another fight.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Agreed.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Starting this week, can we divide appointments and bills? Not "help me," but actual ownership.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I can take pharmacy refills and bills. For appointments, I can do Fridays and any appointment after 3 p.m.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: That helps, but her cardiologist only has mornings.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Then maybe I take the next morning appointment and use PTO. I can't do every one, but I can do the next one.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Okay. That would mean a lot.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: And can we make the calendar assign a person's name? If it says "Mom cardiology - Owen," I won't miss it.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I hate that I have to manage the system, but yes, assigning names is better than guessing.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I can create the shared spreadsheet tonight. You don't have to.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Really?

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Yes. I will add columns for appointment, bill, owner, due date, and status.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: That would help. I am still hurt that it took Mom missing an appointment for this to happen.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I get that. I am sorry I assumed you had it covered.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Thank you.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: I also need you not to bring Aunt Carla into every conversation as proof that I'm failing.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Fair. I bring her up because I feel blamed by the family, but I can see how it lands as me blaming you.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Exactly.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Can we tell Aunt Carla together that appointments will have assigned owners now?

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Yes. Let's send it from both of us.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: Good. I don't want to be the family customer-service department anymore.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: You shouldn't have to be.

turns.speaker: Nina
turns.text: I feel a little relieved. Still tired, but relieved.

turns.speaker: Owen
turns.text: Me too. I'll make the spreadsheet after dinner and send you the link.
